content.title: First Read Rule
body: If this is the agent's first time reading this README for the current work mission, read the entire README before opening deeper docs, following local routes, or relying on this corpus.

A first full pass is required once per current work mission, after a context reset, after a handoff or resume, or after the task materially changes. It is not required at every checkpoint in the same active mission.

After the first full pass, use the routing sections below to decide which local docs to reread or inspect next. At later checkpoints, reread only route-relevant sections unless the active `.core.*` route, roadmap, audit, or local stop condition requires a broader reread.
Do not treat this README as authority over App Behavior, architecture, implementation plans, proof, source, repo facts, product decisions, or explicit user instruction.
Purpose: Index for the human-readable UX schema model and its file, artifact, requirement, and mapping concepts.
Audience: Agents and engineers authoring UX JSON, creating generators, or translating App Behavior docs into schema-compatible artifacts.
Keep In Sync With: JSON Schema files, examples, and authoring standards for App Behavior corpuses.
Update When: The schema model, folder layout, current/desired separation, or mapping behavior changes.
Does Not Cover: Machine-readable schema definitions in full detail.

content.title: Model
body: This folder explains the UX schema system in human terms. Use it before writing or revising production UX JSON.
Files are prefixed in recommended reading order.

content.title: Routing
body: - understanding App Behavior schema concepts, file/folder shape, artifact envelopes, requirement items, current/desired separation, mappings, drift, baselines, and behavior threads
- 14 through 17 for runtime drift, confirmation baselines, behavior threads, and full coverage taxonomy
- app-behavior-shared-specs/README.doc.json and app-behavior-defaults/README.doc.json for dense shared/default behavior models
- the agent can name the model concept, correct artifact role, relationship/mapping shape, and validation expectation

content.title: Route Intent Guidance
body: Use child README routes before model leaf docs when the task belongs to defaults or shared specs.
- For repeated low-risk behavior defaults, maturity rules, or frontend default catalogs, read `app-behavior-defaults/README.doc.json` first.
- For shared App Behavior homes, route references, provisional shared specs, participating-route indexes, or shared-spec maturity, read `app-behavior-shared-specs/README.doc.json` first.
- For core model lifecycle, confirmation baseline model, or behavior lifecycle model, stay in this README and follow its direct model routes.
Stop when the agent can state whether the task is core model, defaults, or shared-spec work, which child README or leaf doc owns the next route, and what behavior authority is still required.

content.title: Confirmation Baseline Model
body: - `15-confirmation-baseline.doc.json` defines row-level commit provenance for UX behavior/spec rows.
- Confirmation baselines answer when a row was last audited against a repo commit; they do not replace product confirmation or runtime alignment records.
- When runtime evidence promotes or reconfirms behavior, record the confirmation baseline plus any staleness or re-verification trigger that would force a future re-check.

content.title: Behavior Lifecycle Model
body: App Behavior rows and behavior-thread containers may record observed/current, intended/target, ideal/future, drift, pending, and verified/proven behavior, but the status and authority role must be explicit.

Source/runtime evidence discovered during investigation should be captured in the App Behavior corpus or routed to a bounded gap, not left only in route inventories, proof notes, audit notes, or chat.
Runtime drift ledgers are routing and triage surfaces. They are not the final behavior authority.
Ideal/future behavior discovered during planning should be recorded as non-current and out of scope unless explicit product/user authority expands the current mission.
